reasoning:
The reference class is stock price forecasting over short-to-medium horizons (7, 30, and 90 days). Base rate of a stock being higher after these periods is generally ~52-55% due to long-run equity risk premiums, but the short-term trend creates deviations. 

The historical price tool gives a statistical probability of 0.59 based on recent trends and seasonal factors. 
EG stock suffered a massive drop on Oct 28 due to a Q3 earnings miss, unfavorable reserve development ($539M), and an underwriting loss (combined ratio 103.4%). However, the stock has quickly begun recovering, rising from $303 on Oct 28 to $324.11 on Nov 7. Several analysts cut their price targets, but all updated targets found in the search (Raymond James at $350, KBW at $400) remain well above the current price of $324.11. InvestingPro also assesses it as slightly undervalued. The company is receiving some cash infusions from renewal rights sales ($301M).

Given the stock is rebounding, analysts maintain targets above the current price, and standard stock drift implies >50% probability, the likelihood of being above $324.11 is better than a coin flip. The momentum is upwards right now. The statistical model estimates 0.59 but this is mechanically derived. Due to the recent major negative shock (which adds volatility and downside risk if the market isn't fully convinced the reserve development is over), I will slightly shrink the model's 0.59 towards 0.5. Short term (Nov 16) is highly subject to immediate momentum (which points up), but I'll set it at 0.56. For Dec 9 and Feb 7, the target prices of $350-$400 provide a stronger pull as the market process digests the earnings misstep, so I'll put those at 0.58. It is unusual for my probabilities to not regress strongly to 0.5 over time, but the analyst targets are very high relative to the current price, indicating fundamental undervaluation that should correct upwards over the 3-month horizon.
